rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type annexes using only visible features and provided bounds.
Use eq declarations to define boolean range predicates before referencing them in guarantees.
generic_rules: Express constraints over visible inputs/outputs, never implementation-private paths.
Define reusable boolean predicates with eq to simplify complex guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use annex agree {** ... **}; for component type contracts.
Declare types as int, real, or bool—never integer or boolean.
Guarantees must reference variables in scope, not AADL properties or unit tokens.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid using subcomponent ports directly in component type annexes.
